title: The Pawnee Eagleton Tip Off Classic

jokes.text: That's the sexiest thing you've ever said to me.
jokes.setup: Ben adds jokes slamming Eagleton to text
jokes.punchline: Leslie finds political attacks sexually arousing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her obsession with politics extends to finding it arousing. Well-structured reveal of character quirk.

jokes.text: Look. Am I proud of it? Yes. Because Eagleton sucks. But is it the classiest move? Yes, because Eagleton sucks. Would I do it again? Yes, because Eagleton sucks.
jokes.setup: Leslie questions herself about attacking Eagleton
jokes.punchline: Every answer is 'yes' with the same justification
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating structure with perfect Leslie logic. The repetitive 'because Eagleton sucks' becomes funnier each time, showing her single-minded obsession.

jokes.text: She thinks she's so great because she won the Miss Indiana Beauty Pageant. Last year. While she was in office. And pregnant.
jokes.setup: Leslie complains about Ingrid being stuck-up
jokes.punchline: Each detail makes Ingrid's accomplishment more impressive
jokes.explanation: Great escalating reveal where each addition makes Ingrid seem more accomplished, undermining Leslie's complaint.

jokes.text: Oh, no, these are pesticide-free. I ate one of these for breakfast this morning, and I found a worm in it. So, I bet somebody feels pretty stupid right now.
jokes.setup: Ingrid rejects apples as having pesticides
jokes.punchline: Leslie's proof they're pesticide-free actually makes them worse
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection where Leslie's attempt to prove superiority backfires spectacularly, with her oblivious confidence making it funnier.

jokes.text: These are made of Swarovski crystal, and they're worth $700,000.
jokes.setup: Simple comparison between town wagers - apples vs oranges
jokes.punchline: Eagleton's oranges are worth a fortune
jokes.explanation: Perfect Eagleton absurdity - they can't even make a simple wager without being ridiculously extravagant.

jokes.text: I'd pop a Xanax and ask my butler to help me out.
jokes.setup: Asked what she'd do as an Eagletonian
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical wealthy person response
jokes.explanation: Functional joke relying on rich person stereotypes. Gets a laugh but not particularly original.

jokes.text: But when you tell 'em it's time to change their oil, they ask, 'Extra virgin or white truffle?'
jokes.setup: Eagletonians are out of touch
jokes.punchline: They confuse motor oil with fancy cooking oil
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures the wealth/cluelessness dynamic. Economical and well-structured.

jokes.text: Unless, of course, you're an Eagletonian, and then you'll be too busy polishing your monocle at the caviar store.
jokes.setup: See you at the game
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated wealthy person activities
jokes.explanation: More broad rich person stereotyping. Monocle and caviar store are predictable choices.

jokes.text: We only ever subscribe to two magazines, Reader's Digest and Ebony. Ebony was due to a clerical error, but it ended up being an interesting year of reading.
jokes.setup: Ron describes his family's magazine subscriptions
jokes.punchline: Accidentally subscribing to Ebony was educational
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - his deadpan delivery of accidentally learning about different perspectives. Character-specific and genuinely surprising.

jokes.text: She got me off the Williams-Sonoma mailing list, but then I just signed back up again. Hooked on their oven mitts. I need my mitts on those mitts.
jokes.setup: Jerry explains Donna's skill with mailing lists
jokes.punchline: His addiction to oven mitts with pun
jokes.explanation: Solid Jerry joke with decent pun. Character-appropriate enthusiasm for mundane domestic items.

jokes.text: According to her Twitter feed, she got coffee five minutes ago. Wait, now she's Ustreaming her walk back. She should be here in three, two, one.
jokes.setup: Tracking Donna's location
jokes.punchline: Precise countdown from social media stalking
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment showing his social media obsession and creepy tracking abilities.

jokes.text: Hashtag quest for coffee, blowin' up my timeline. You get my snapchat about it?
jokes.setup: Donna arrives after Tom's tracking
jokes.punchline: She's fully aware and participating in over-documentation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna - she's not just tracked, she's actively promoting her mundane activities.

jokes.text: Hey, Dr. Buttons-- I mean, my old calculator. It doesn't have a name.
jokes.setup: Chris catches calculator Ben throws
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals he names his calculator
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment - his optimistic anthropomorphizing of objects revealed through awkward cover-up.

jokes.text: Butch Count-sidy and the Sum-dance Kid, together again.
jokes.setup: Reuniting with Ben for accounting work
jokes.punchline: Math puns on Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid
jokes.explanation: Solid Chris-style wordplay combining his enthusiasm with clever math puns.

jokes.text: Auditing bros! Yeah, and auditing sistah. Let's do this.
jokes.setup: Chris gets excited about auditing
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic bro speak corrected for inclusion
jokes.explanation: Minor Chris moment showing his inclusive enthusiasm, though not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: If you make your omelet out of Faberge eggs, you just might... Be an Eagletonian.
jokes.setup: Tom's Jeff Foxworthy-style routine about Eagleton
jokes.punchline: Absurdly expensive breakfast ingredient
jokes.explanation: Decent Tom joke combining his showbiz aspirations with Eagleton mockery. Format works well.

jokes.text: I would get one in Pawnee, but fun fact-- our only baby store is attached to a chemical refinery.
jokes.setup: Andy explains why they're going to Bloomington for baby supplies
jokes.punchline: Pawnee's baby store location is hazardous
jokes.explanation: Good Pawnee-is-terrible joke with dark implications delivered cheerfully.

jokes.text: I made a special road trip mix CD for us. I hope you like Tori Amos.
jokes.setup: Andy prepared music for the trip
jokes.punchline: His choice is completely wrong for his character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - his sincere effort results in completely inappropriate musical choice.

jokes.text: Trunk.
jokes.setup: Andy asks where else he would sit besides driver's seat
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact suggestion he ride in trunk
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan delivery with maximum economy

jokes.text: We don't really like to talk about money. We find a little gauche.
jokes.setup: Asked about budget deficits at a budget meeting
jokes.punchline: She finds discussing money inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good Ingrid character moment showing her privileged disconnect from practical reality.

jokes.text: Are you tipping me? Is that 100...Euros?
jokes.setup: Ingrid hands Chris something
jokes.punchline: She casually tips with foreign currency
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag showing Eagleton's casual wealth and international pretensions.

jokes.text: I live for the grid. It's where I meet 40% of my jump-offs.
jokes.setup: Ron wants to get off the grid
jokes.punchline: Donna's romantic life depends on social media
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna - combining her love of technology with casual attitude toward dating.

jokes.text: Grid, grid, grid, grid, grid, grid, grid-- If you help me get off the grid, I will let both of you go home early. No grid, no grid, no grid, no grid.
jokes.setup: Tom and Donna chant about the grid
jokes.punchline: They instantly reverse their chant for early dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment showing how quickly they'll abandon principles for personal gain.

jokes.text: Yeah, an empty Sprite can, an old man's fingernail in my pocket, and a picture of Patrick Ewing on a roller coaster. I'm all set.
jokes.setup: Asked if she has everything for orientation
jokes.punchline: Completely random, useless items
jokes.explanation: Excellent April absurdism - the specificity and randomness of the items is perfectly in character and genuinely surprising.

jokes.text: I'm April Ludgate Kevorkian.
jokes.setup: April introduces herself to college guide
jokes.punchline: She adds the assisted suicide doctor's name
jokes.explanation: Perfect April dark humor - casually adding a morbid reference to her name in a cheerful college setting.

jokes.text: Sure you wanna leave me here with her? What if I'm a murderer?
jokes.setup: Andy leaves April with the college guide
jokes.punchline: April suggests she might kill the nice guide
jokes.explanation: Good April moment - her casual dark humor contrasted with the wholesome college setting.

jokes.text: Their debt-to-equity ratio is ridonkulous. What a hilarious word. It reminds me of a ridiculous donkey.
jokes.setup: Chris describes Eagleton's financial problems
jokes.punchline: He gets distracted by the silly word he created
jokes.explanation: Typical Chris moment - his enthusiasm for wordplay distracts from serious business.

jokes.text: Now that is the sexiest thing you've ever said to me.
jokes.setup: Ben reports Eagleton's financial disaster
jokes.punchline: Leslie again finds political advantage arousing
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces Leslie's character while showing escalation of her political arousal.

jokes.text: Do you like apples? We just watched this movie two nights ago, so-- Do you like apples? I don't wanna do this. How about them apples?
jokes.setup: Leslie wants to recreate Good Will Hunting scene
jokes.punchline: Ben's reluctance and her persistence make it awkward
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Leslie's inability to read social situations when she's excited.

jokes.text: Passing the buck-- the last refuge of the cowardly and black-hearted.
jokes.setup: Paul says he just delivers mail, doesn't decide who gets it
jokes.punchline: Ron's dramatic overreaction to bureaucratic excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - his hatred of bureaucracy expressed in overly dramatic, archaic language.

jokes.text: These are the first bibs I've ever seen without Dale Earnhardt Jr.'s face on them.
jokes.setup: Andy shopping for baby supplies
jokes.punchline: Pawnee baby products feature NASCAR drivers
jokes.explanation: Good regional humor showing Pawnee's blue-collar character through baby product design.

jokes.text: Um, I was at orientation, but it was lame, so I left. And then I kept walking, and now here I am.
jokes.setup: Andy finds April at baby store instead of orientation
jokes.punchline: April's casual abandonment of major life decision
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - treating a major life decision with complete indifference and wandering off.

jokes.text: We came from our bankruptcy brunch. And Michael Buble played, but he ran a little long.
jokes.setup: Apologizing for being late to budget meeting
jokes.punchline: They had entertainment at their bankruptcy announcement
jokes.explanation: Excellent Eagleton absurdity - they make bankruptcy announcement into a fancy event with live entertainment. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Sure, let's not have brunch... like animals.
jokes.setup: Someone questions if bankruptcy brunch was appropriate
jokes.punchline: Ingrid equates not brunching with being uncivilized
jokes.explanation: Great Ingrid moment - her priorities are completely backwards but delivered with conviction.

jokes.text: Uh, you filled the public pools with bottled water? Total body hydration.
jokes.setup: Ben questions expensive expenditure
jokes.punchline: Ingrid's spa-like justification for public pools
jokes.explanation: Great example of Eagleton's wasteful spending justified with wellness nonsense.

jokes.text: We purchased HBO for the whole town. I'm sorry. You spent government money on a TV subscription? It's not TV.
jokes.setup: Ingrid explains HBO purchase
jokes.punchline: She uses HBO's old slogan to justify waste
jokes.explanation: Clever use of HBO's marketing slogan to defend inappropriate government spending.

jokes.text: Couldn't cook 'em fast enough. The last ten were still in the shell.
jokes.setup: Ron's eating record continues
jokes.punchline: He ate raw eggs in shell from impatience
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the egg story. The visual of Ron eating raw eggs in shells because he's impatient is both disgusting and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: That's too much information.
jokes.setup: Ron explains his privacy needs
jokes.punchline: JJ's understated response to Ron's oversharing
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Ron's absurd story, but relatively simple.

jokes.text: As you remember, I'm April, and this is Ann, my 65-year-old grandmother.
jokes.setup: April introduces herself and Andy to college guide
jokes.punchline: She claims Andy is her elderly grandmother
jokes.explanation: Good April prank - forcing Andy into an awkward role-play while maintaining straight face.

jokes.text: Mee-maw, God, no one uses the word hip anymore.
jokes.setup: Andy tries to play along as grandmother
jokes.punchline: April corrects her 'grandmother's' outdated slang
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the grandmother bit with April's commitment to the charade.

jokes.text: Sometimes I wish you went down on the Titanic with all your sorority sisters.
jokes.setup: April continues grandmother roleplay
jokes.punchline: Wishes death on her 'grandmother'
jokes.explanation: Excellent April dark humor - staying in character while expressing her actual feelings about forced social situations.

jokes.text: How are your local graveyards? I have some planning to do.
jokes.setup: Asked what she wants to see around campus
jokes.punchline: She's interested in death-related locations
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - turning innocent campus tour question into something morbid.

jokes.text: 'You blew it. Super hard. Complete buffoonery. It's hilarious.' And 'You deserve it.'
jokes.setup: Leslie reads her presentation slides
jokes.punchline: Her 'helpful' presentation is just insults
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her inability to resist gloating even when trying to help.

jokes.text: Whatever, blondie. Your butler made your bed, now you gotta lie in it.
jokes.setup: Negotiating with Eagleton
jokes.punchline: Mixing idioms with butler reference
jokes.explanation: Decent Leslie moment combining her competitive nature with class-based insults.

jokes.text: You don't kick a dressage horse after a failed pas de deux.
jokes.setup: Ingrid responds to Leslie's aggression
jokes.punchline: Her saying is completely pretentious and nonsensical
jokes.explanation: Great Ingrid moment - creating a completely fake fancy saying that sounds sophisticated but makes no sense.

jokes.text: Oh, I've been taking pictures of you all day for my new Facebook album: 'Ronswan: Gettin' off the grid.'
jokes.setup: Ron trying to get off the grid
jokes.punchline: Tom has been documenting it online
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic joke - Tom completely defeats Ron's privacy goal while thinking he's helping. Perfect character dynamics.

jokes.text: You just vined your first selfie, Ron. And I'm vining you vine your selfie.
jokes.setup: Ron accidentally uses social media
jokes.punchline: Tom creates recursive social media documentation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Tom's social media obsession creating a feedback loop of documentation.

jokes.text: The world is a nightmare.
jokes.setup: Ron experiencing modern technology
jokes.punchline: His dramatic assessment of social media
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron reaction - his existential horror at technology delivered with characteristic brevity and intensity.

jokes.text: Youngsters? Am I 65 years old?
jokes.setup: Andy uses outdated language
jokes.punchline: April references her fake grandmother age
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the grandmother bit while also mocking Andy's language choices.

jokes.text: Why do people keep assuming I'm ready to catch things?
jokes.setup: Someone throws something at Chris again
jokes.punchline: His frustrated observation about people throwing things
jokes.explanation: Good running gag about Chris's poor reflexes and people's assumptions.

jokes.text: 'Eagleton: No money, mo' problems.' Fantastic, right? I made enough of them for everybody to wear at the game.
jokes.setup: Leslie shows her t-shirt design
jokes.punchline: Biggie Smalls reference applied to municipal finance
jokes.explanation: Decent Leslie joke combining her love of political attacks with pop culture references.

jokes.text: I have a PhD from the Sorbonne. For what? Wearing Chanel suits? There was a fashion component, yes.
jokes.setup: Ingrid defends her intelligence
jokes.punchline: Her PhD actually did involve fashion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment where Leslie's sarcastic guess turns out to be partially correct.

jokes.text: I speak for all of us in all matters. That is what you tacitly agreed to when you married me.
jokes.setup: Ben says he never agreed to boycott Eagleton
jokes.punchline: Leslie's authoritarian interpretation of marriage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie control-freak moment, delivered with complete sincerity about marriage dynamics.

jokes.text: What, Ann? I just stole your phone and texted every guy in it that the baby was theirs. It's not that big of a deal.
jokes.setup: Andy asks what April did
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of causing relationship chaos
jokes.explanation: Excellent April moment - her casual delivery of causing maximum chaos with the understated 'not that big of a deal.'

jokes.text: And I've met Guy Fieri, Ann. So gross!
jokes.setup: April says Andy would be the worst person she'd meet
jokes.punchline: Uses Guy Fieri as benchmark for awful people
jokes.explanation: Good escalation using a pop culture reference April would genuinely find repulsive.

jokes.text: There's no way he is in high school. And I'm better at French horn too, Eric.
jokes.setup: Leslie complains about opposing player
jokes.punchline: Player responds with random musical boast
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist moment but relatively minor compared to episode's stronger jokes.

jokes.text: I love you, but your solution to every problem is to live inside a mountain.
jokes.setup: Ron suggests moving to Yucca Mountain
jokes.punchline: Diane points out his pattern of extreme solutions
jokes.explanation: Excellent character observation that reveals Ron's consistent extremist approach to problems with loving exasperation.

jokes.text: I hate their stupid beautiful faces and their stupid shiny hair.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why she hates Eagletonians
jokes.punchline: Admits they're attractive while calling it stupid
jokes.explanation: Leslie's jealousy disguised as hatred with contradictory compliments

jokes.text: No one bailed me out when I got arrested for arson.
jokes.setup: Citizen complaining about bailing out Eagleton
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of serious crime
jokes.explanation: Good dark Pawnee humor - casual criminal admission in political context.

jokes.text: What's next-- merging a man with his dog?
jokes.setup: Opposing town merger
jokes.punchline: Slippery slope to human-animal merger
jokes.explanation: Typical town hall absurdity but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: They're a little-- oh, what's the word? Have-no-money.
jokes.setup: Bertram describing Pawnee citizens
jokes.punchline: His pretentious pause before saying 'poor'
jokes.explanation: Good Eagleton snobbery moment with his affected search for a 'polite' way to say poor.

jokes.text: They're a town of angels-- greasy, denim-clad angels, but angels nonetheless.
jokes.setup: Ingrid defending Pawnee's generosity
jokes.punchline: Her compliment includes class-based insults
jokes.explanation: Good Ingrid moment showing her inability to compliment without revealing her prejudices.

jokes.text: It looks like the original phone from the movie Wall Street. I think it's just the original phone, period.
jokes.setup: Zoe comments on old phone's appearance
jokes.punchline: Diane suggests it's even older than movie prop
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation joke about the phone's age but relatively minor.

jokes.text: A cell phone belt clip? No, Ron. Oh, my God! Yeah, I can't be seen with you.
jokes.setup: Ron shows off his phone solution
jokes.punchline: Tom's horror at unfashionable accessory
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Tom's fashion consciousness versus Ron's pure practicality.

jokes.text: The thing I'm having is a gut feeling.
jokes.setup: Chris seems to be having a contraction
jokes.punchline: He's having an emotional realization, not a medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on pregnancy terminology for emotional revelation.

jokes.text: All of Pawnee's apples have been recalled-- g. Coli. You mean e. Coli? I wish. Way easy to treat.
jokes.setup: Leslie admits their apples are contaminated
jokes.punchline: The contamination is worse than e. Coli
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback and escalation - the apple situation gets progressively worse, with Leslie's casual delivery making it funnier.

tmdb_overview: Leslie pairs up the Pawnee Parks employees with their Eagleton counterparts. Meanwhile Ben and Chris reunite for an accounting project, and Ann reveals her plans for the future.
insight: Sports comedy escalation carries a solid B-tier episode with 66 jokes in 27 minutes.
